title: AgentSkin signals.json
description: 3-layer rule config for AgentSkin's URL auto-classifier. Project overrides user overrides builtin. Same-id rules in a higher layer REPLACE the lower-layer rule (no deep-merge); "disabled": true in any layer REMOVES the matching lower-layer rule; new id values are APPENDED to the end of the merged set.

$schema.description: Path or URL of this schema file. Adjust to point at wherever you place the schema on disk so editors can autocomplete rule fields.

$comment.description: Free-form note. Ignored by the resolver.

rules.description: Layer-local rules. Merged with the other two layers at load time per the 3-layer priority (project > user > builtin).

rule.description: A single API skin rule. A rule is either ACTIVE (has a `match` clause and a `signals` array) or a DISABLE DIRECTIVE (has `"disabled": true` and no other meaningful fields).

id.description: Unique rule identifier. When this id matches a rule in a lower layer, this rule REPLACES it. Convention: `<family>/<name>` (e.g. `github/repos`).

family.description: API domain family. Used purely for grouping and introspection (see getFamilies()). Has no effect on matching.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in tooling output.

match.description: URL match criteria. Required for ACTIVE rules; omit (along with `signals`) for DISABLE DIRECTIVES.

urlIncludes.description: URL must contain ALL of these substrings (case-insensitive). Use for specific paths like `api.github.com/repos/`.

urlIncludesAny.description: URL must contain AT LEAST ONE of these substrings (case-insensitive). Use for multi-domain families like Reddit (reddit.com OR oauth.reddit.com).

signals.description: Signal keys to preserve from API responses. Order does not affect matching. Required for ACTIVE rules; omit for DISABLE DIRECTIVES.

aliases.description: Key alias renames. snake_case API fields → readable names. Keys are original field names, values are display names.

transforms.description: Optional transformation flags consumed by the skinned output renderer.

priority.description: Match priority bonus. Higher scores win when multiple rules match a URL. Typical builtin values: 90-100.

disabled.description: If true, the matching lower-layer rule (same `id`) is removed from the merged set. A pure disable directive should be `{id: '<id>', disabled: true}` with no other fields. Disable wins over conflicting non-disabled overrides in any other layer (see THREE-LAYER-RULES.md).

allOf.description: Conditional: if `match` is present, `signals` must also be present. Disable directives need neither.
Conditional: if `disabled` is present, neither `match` nor `signals` may also be present (a disable directive is pure).
